meta.title: Freddie Mac Multifamily Outlook
meta.methodology: Freddie Mac's Multifamily Research group publishes a free quarterly outlook covering origination volume, expected debt costs, cap-rate trends, and forecast rent + occupancy by region. Freddie's data is industry-standard for multifamily debt sizing — Freddie + Fannie agency loans are 40-50% of the multifamily debt market. The report frames the macro environment for LIHTC permanent debt assumptions.
meta.important_disclaimer: Numbers below are COHO's best-effort directional estimates from publicly-available Freddie Mac quarterly outlook coverage. For IC memos requiring actual debt cost or cap-rate citation, ALWAYS pull the latest Freddie Mac Multifamily Outlook PDF directly. Treasury yields and lending spreads move daily; the figures below are current as of the report publication date.

macro_debt_environment.narrative_macro: Fed funds target range 4.50-4.75% (after 25bp cuts in March + May 2026). 10y Treasury yield 4.25%, well off the 5.0% peak of late 2024. Curve has steepened modestly; debt environment more favorable for multifamily lending than 12 months ago.
macro_debt_environment.narrative_spread: Freddie Mac Multifamily fixed-rate perm spread over 10y Treasury is approximately 165 bps (down from 200bps mid-2024 as market-clearing prices settled). Implied perm rate for stabilized affordable: ~5.90%. LIHTC deals typically price 25-50bp wider than market-rate due to compliance complexity.

cap_rates.narrative: Cap rates have compressed about 25 bps over the past 6 months as the rate-cut cycle began. Garden-style class-B nationally at 6.25%. Mountain West (Denver, Salt Lake, Phoenix) 25 bps wider at 6.50% — fewer transactions, smaller buyer pool. Workforce LIHTC (mixed 60%/80% AMI) typically trades 50bp wider than market-rate class-B, currently 6.75% nationally.

forecast.narrative: Freddie forecasts 2.5% rent growth nationally in the next 12 months — slight acceleration from current 1.4% as new-supply delivery slows. Occupancy expected to stabilize at 94%. Originations volume expected to grow 8% YoY as cap-rate-compression dynamics restart deal flow.

lihtc_dev_implications.perm_loan_assumption: Use 5.90% as the base case for stabilized perm debt assumption in Deal Calc (down from the 6.5% default). For workforce / 80% AMI tiers add 25bp; for rural CO add 25bp; for stress test add 50-75bp.
lihtc_dev_implications.exit_cap_assumption: Use 6.50% (Mountain West class-B garden) as the base exit cap in Deal Calc Year-15 analysis. For workforce LIHTC use 6.75%. Conservative pricing builds cushion.
lihtc_dev_implications.refinance_assumption: If your Year-15 exit is a refi rather than sale, use the same 5.90% base perm assumption + 25bp execution premium for mature project = 6.15%.
